name: Brihaspati
description: Brihaspati (Sanskrit: बृहस्पति, IAST: Bṛhaspati) is a Hindu god. In the ancient Vedic scriptures, Brihaspati is associated with fire, and the word also refers to a god who counsels the devas and devis (gods and goddesses).[2][3][4] In some later texts, the word refers to the largest planet of the Solar System, Jupiter, and the deity is associated with the planet as a Navagraha.[2][5]

infobox.Mount: chariot drawn by eight white horses
infobox.Children: Bhanumati, Raka, Archishmati, Mahamati, Mahishmati, Sinivali and Habishmati from Subha Kesari, Kushadhvaja, Kacha, another 4 sons and Romashaa from Tara; Bharadvaja and Revati from Mammata
